activities_projects.title: The Voting Line Roleplay
activities_projects.objective: Understand the concept of Universal Adult Franchise
steps: Assign roles to students: a teacher, a trader, a domestic help, and a student
Line everyone up in a single queue to cast their vote
Discuss how, in the eyes of the law, every person in that line has one vote of equal value
Ask how the characters feel being equal in the voting line
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes that democracy gives every adult equal voting power regardless of their job or wealth
activities_projects.evaluation: Participation in roleplay and understanding of 'One person, one vote' (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Midday Meal Experience Chart
activities_projects.objective: Identify the benefits of the Midday Meal scheme in schools
steps: Draw a scene of children from different backgrounds sitting and eating together
List two benefits of the scheme (e.g., poor children get food, improved attendance)
Identify which state in India started this scheme first (Tamil Nadu)
Discuss how eating together helps in reducing caste prejudice
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands how government schemes promote social equality
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct identification of the scheme's history and social benefits (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Respecting Dignity Case Study
activities_projects.objective: Learn that equality is about being treated with respect
materials: Story of Omprakash Valmiki or the Ansaris from the textbook
steps: Read the story of how a person was treated unfairly in the chapter
Write down one word that describes how the person felt (e.g., hurt, insulted)
Explain why it is wrong to treat someone differently based on their religion or caste
Define 'Dignity' in your own simple words
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Identifies that violating someone's dignity is a violation of equality
activities_projects.evaluation: Ability to empathize with the characters and define dignity (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Indian Constitution Flipbook
activities_projects.objective: Identify the four key provisions of equality in the Constitution
steps: Create a card for each rule: 1. Equality before law, 2. No discrimination, 3. Access to public places, 4. Untouchability is abolished
Draw a small symbol for each (e.g., a scale for law, a playground for public places)
Flip through the cards to memorize the rights given to every Indian citizen
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Memorizes the basic legal pillars of equality in India
activities_projects.evaluation: Inclusion of all four key provisions correctly (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Public vs. Private Facility Tour
activities_projects.objective: Observe differences in access to facilities
steps: Think about a local Government Hospital and a Private Hospital
Compare things like: Cleanliness, Waiting time, and Cost
Discuss if everyone in India has equal access to high-quality healthcare
Identify if this is a form of inequality based on money
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes economic inequality in daily life services
activities_projects.evaluation: Observation of differences and their link to economic status (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Civil Rights Movement Story
activities_projects.objective: Understand that the struggle for equality is global
steps: Read the short story of Rosa Parks from the chapter
Identify what she refused to do (give up her seat on a bus)
Write down the year the Civil Rights Act was passed in the USA (1964)
Explain how one small act can lead to a big change for equality
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Connects Indian democratic struggles with international movements for equality
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct facts about the US Civil Rights Movement (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Poster: 'All Are Equal'
steps: Write 'Article 15' in big letters
Draw people of different religions, genders, and castes standing together
Write the slogan: 'No citizen shall be discriminated against'
Decorate it for the classroom wall
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Visualizes the non-discriminatory nature of the Indian Constitution
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity and use of constitutional language (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Daily Equality Checklist
activities_projects.objective: Identify equal treatment in the classroom
steps: Tick 'Yes' or 'No' for: Does everyone have the same desk? Does everyone get to play during break? Is the teacher fair to everyone?
If anything is 'No', discuss how it can be fixed
Write one rule for the class to ensure everyone feels included
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies the concept of equality to their immediate surroundings
activities_projects.evaluation: Thoughtfulness in the checklist and suggested rules (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: B.R. Ambedkar’s Quote Analysis
materials: A printed quote of Dr. B.R. Ambedkar about self-respect
steps: Read the quote provided in the textbook
Discuss why Ambedkar felt that equality is not just a law but a way of living with pride
Write one sentence on why we should respect ourselves and others
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Learns about the contribution of key leaders in the fight for equality
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity of thought in the written sentence (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Equality Word Match
steps: Match words like: Constitution, Democracy, Equality, Dignity
With meanings like: Government by the people, Rulebook of a country, Being treated the same, Self-respect
Check answers with a partner
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Reinforces key terms related to Indian Democracy
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy in matching terms with their definitions (10 marks)
